title: Aleuritia khasiana
intro: Aleuritia khasiana, also known as Himalayan sarsaparilla or Khasarab, is a plant species belonging to the Smilacaceae family, traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in treating various ailments such as fever, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Aleuritia khasiana include saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Aleuritia khasiana include decoctions, infusions, and powders, often used to treat fever, cough, and skin infections.
